12-Week Base Training Block
Stokoe Fit · Hybrid Athlete Programme

12-WEEK
BASE PLAN

Duration 12 Weeks
Sessions / Week 7 Days
Phases 4 × 3 Weeks
Modalities Strength + Endurance
Intensity Metric RPE + Heart Rate Zone
Legend:
RPE = Rate of Perceived Exertion (1–10)
Z1 = Easy / Conversational (<65% HRmax)
Z2 = Aerobic (65–75%)
Z3 = Tempo (75–85%)
Z4 = Threshold (85–92%)
Z5 = Max (>92%)
Week:
Phase 1 · Weeks 1–3 FOUNDATION
Phase Focus

Build aerobic base, establish movement patterns, prime tendons and connective tissue. All lifts at moderate load. Cardio stays in Z1–Z2. No ego lifting — quality reps only. Learn the rhythm of hybrid training.

01
INTRODUCTION WEEK
Establish patterns · RPE 6–7 lifts · Z1–Z2 cardio throughout · Volume is moderate — don't chase intensity
Est. Run Volume
22km
Lifting Days
6days
Long Run
10km
Bike
40min
Avg RPE Lifts
6.5
D1 CHEST / ARMS + TEMPO RUN Hybrid
Strength — Chest & Arms
Incline Barbell Bench Press4×8RPE 6
Flat Dumbbell Press3×10RPE 6
Cable Chest Fly (low-to-high)3×12RPE 6
EZ-Bar Curl3×10RPE 6
Hammer Curl3×12RPE 6
Rope Tricep Pushdown3×12RPE 6
Overhead Tricep Extension (DB)3×12RPE 6
Cardio — Tempo/Track Run
Tempo Intervals — 4×800m
Warm-up: 1.5km easy jog Z1
Main: 4 × 800m at comfortably hard pace Z3 — 90s walk/jog recovery between each
Cool-down: 1km easy Z1
Total: ~7km

Luke Note: Keep the bench honest — this is week 1. Touch and go is fine but control the eccentric. For the tempo reps, you should be able to hold form but not able to hold a conversation.

D2 BACK / SHOULDERS + EASY RUN Hybrid
Strength — Back & Shoulders
Barbell Overhead Press4×8RPE 6
Pull-Ups (bodyweight)4×6–8RPE 6
Barbell Pendlay Row4×8RPE 6
Seated Cable Row (wide grip)3×12RPE 6
Dumbbell Lateral Raise4×15RPE 6
Face Pull (cable)3×15RPE 5
Cardio — Easy Run
Easy Aerobic Run — 5km
Fully conversational pace Z1Z2
Heart rate: keep below 75% HRmax. This is recovery + aerobic base work.
D3 LEGS + BIKE Hybrid
Strength — Legs
Back Squat4×6RPE 7
Romanian Deadlift3×10RPE 6
Leg Press3×12RPE 6
Seated Leg Curl3×12RPE 6
Standing Calf Raise4×15RPE 6
Cardio — Bike
Steady State Aerobic — 40min
Comfortable resistance, smooth pedal stroke Z2
Legs will be fatigued post-lifting — keep effort honest, not heroic.

Luke Note: Don't skip the bike because your legs are pumped. The double stimulus teaches your body to work aerobically under muscular fatigue — that's the hybrid point.

D4 CHEST / BACK + EASY RUN Hybrid
Strength — Chest & Back (Push/Pull)
Flat Barbell Bench Press4×8RPE 7
Weighted Pull-Ups4×6RPE 7
DB Incline Press3×10RPE 6
Single-Arm DB Row3×10eaRPE 6
Cable Chest Fly (high-to-low)3×12RPE 6
Lat Pulldown (close grip)3×12RPE 6
Cardio — Easy Run
Easy Run — 5km
Z1Z2 Conversational. No watch-staring. Run by feel.
D5 SHOULDERS / ARMS + INCLINE RUN Hybrid
Strength — Shoulders & Arms
Seated DB Shoulder Press4×10RPE 6
Lateral Raise (cable)4×15RPE 6
Rear Delt Fly (pec dec)3×15RPE 6
Barbell Curl3×10RPE 6
Skull Crushers (EZ bar)3×10RPE 6
Incline DB Curl3×12RPE 6
Tricep Bench Dips3×15RPE 5
Cardio — Easy/Incline Run
Incline Treadmill Walk/Run — 20min
5–8% incline, moderate pace Z2
Builds posterior chain strength + aerobic fitness without pounding.
D6 LONG RUN + ACTIVE RECOVERY Recovery
Cardio — Long Run
Long Slow Run — 10km
Pace: Easy conversational Z1Z2
Never let HR drift above Z2. If it does — slow down or walk.
Goal: Time on feet. Aerobic adaptation. Not performance.
Active Recovery
Post-Run Mobility + Foam Roll — 20min
Hamstrings · Hip flexors · IT band · Calves · Thoracic spine
Optional: 10min cold shower or contrast therapy.

Luke Note: The long run is your aerobic engine builder. Never race it. 80% of your running should feel embarrassingly slow. Embrace it.

D7 LEGS + JUMP ROPE / SWIM Hybrid
Strength — Legs (Unilateral Focus)
Goblet Squat (tempo 3-1-1)4×10RPE 6
Bulgarian Split Squat (DB)3×8eaRPE 6
Walking Lunges3×12eaRPE 6
Leg Extension3×15RPE 6
Nordic Curl / Hamstring Curl3×8RPE 6
Seated Calf Raise3×20RPE 5
Cardio — Jump Rope or Swim
Jump Rope: 3 × 3min AMRAP / OR Swim: 400m steady
Jump Rope: 3 rounds × 3min on, 1min rest Z3
Swim: 400m easy steady state as cool-down Z2
Choose based on recovery needs — swim if legs are thrashed.
02
BUILDING FAMILIARITY
Add 1–2 reps or small load increase · RPE 7 lifts · Same run paces — slightly more volume · Stay in Z1–Z2

25km

Z1Z2 Keep it conversational.
D5SHOULDERS / ARMS + INCLINE RUNHybrid
Strength — Shoulders & Arms

D6LONG RUN + ACTIVE RECOVERYRecovery
Cardio — Long Run
Long Run — 12km
Z1Z2 Easy and consistent. Add 2km from week 1. Walk when needed.
Active Recovery
Mobility + Foam Roll — 25min
Focus: hip flexors, glutes, calves, thoracic spine. Optional contrast shower.
D7LEGS + JUMP ROPE / SWIMHybrid
Strength — Legs (Unilateral)
Goblet Squat (3-1-1 tempo)4×10 (+2kg)RPE 7
Bulgarian Split Squat3×8ea (+2kg)RPE 7
Walking Lunges3×14eaRPE 7
Leg Extension3×15RPE 7
Nordic Curl3×8RPE 7
Seated Calf Raise3×20RPE 6
Cardio — Jump Rope or Swim
Jump Rope: 4×3min / Swim: 500m
Z3 Jump rope or steady swim based on recovery. Form over speed.
03
DELOAD / CONSOLIDATION
Volume drops 20% · Same weights, fewer sets · Run shorter · Let adaptations from W1–2 absorb · Sleep + nutrition priority
Est. Run Volume
18km
Lifting Days
6days
Long Run
10km
Bike
30min

6.0
D1CHEST / ARMS + TEMPO RUNHybrid
Strength — Chest & Arms (Deload)
Incline Barbell Bench Press3×8 (–10%)RPE 6
Flat Dumbbell Press3×8RPE 6
Cable Chest Fly2×12RPE 5
EZ-Bar Curl3×8RPE 6
Rope Tricep Pushdown3×12RPE 6
Cardio — Tempo Run (Reduced)
3×800m Easy Tempo
Z3 3 reps only. Feel fresh. 90s recovery.

Luke Note: Deload weeks make you stronger. Pros never skip them. Growth happens during recovery, not during the sessions.

Z1 Pure easy. Just move.

Steady Bike — 30min
Z2 Easy spin. Flush the legs.
D4CHEST / BACK + EASY RUNHybrid
Strength — Push/Pull (Deload)

Weighted Pull-Ups3×5RPE 6
DB Incline Press2×10RPE 6
Single-Arm DB Row2×10eaRPE 6
Cardio
Easy Run — 4km
Z1 Enjoy it. You earned a light day.
D5SHOULDERS / ARMS + INCLINE RUNHybrid
Strength — Shoulders & Arms (Deload)
Seated DB Press3×8 (–10%)RPE 6
Lateral Raise3×12RPE 5
Barbell Curl3×8RPE 6

Easy Incline Walk — 20min
5% incline Z1 Walk only. Recovery focus.
D6LONG RUN + ACTIVE RECOVERYRecovery
Cardio — Long Run (Deload)
Long Run — 10km
Z1Z2 Same as week 1. No progression this week.
Recovery
Full Mobility Session — 30min
Yoga flow or full body stretch. Ice bath optional. Prioritise sleep tonight.
D7LEGS + JUMP ROPE / SWIMHybrid
Strength — Legs (Deload)
Goblet Squat3×8RPE 5
Bulgarian Split Squat2×8eaRPE 5
Walking Lunges2×10eaRPE 5
Leg Curl2×12RPE 5
Cardio
Swim: 400m easy / Jump Rope: 2×3min
Z2 Prefer the swim this week — joint-friendly deload option.
Phase 2 · Weeks 4–6 BUILD
Phase Focus

Progressive overload increases. Run volume climbs. Tempo work gets more demanding. Introduce Z3–Z4 running efforts. Lifts hit RPE 8. Nutrition and sleep become even more critical.

04
PHASE 2 KICKOFF — PUSH HARDER
Load increases on all lifts · Tempo intervals longer · Easy runs hit 6km · Long run extends to 14km · Introduce Z3 efforts
Est. Run Volume
30km
Lifting Days
6days
Long Run
14km
Bike
50min
Avg RPE Lifts
7.5
D1CHEST / ARMS + TEMPO RUNHybrid
Strength — Chest & Arms
Incline Barbell Bench Press5×6RPE 8
Flat Dumbbell Press4×10RPE 7
Cable Fly + Squeeze at peak3×12RPE 7
Close-Grip Bench Press3×8RPE 7
Preacher Curl (EZ bar)3×10RPE 7
Hammer Curl3×12RPE 7
Rope Tricep Pushdown4×12RPE 7
Cardio — Tempo Run
6×800m Tempo Intervals
Warm-up: 2km easy Z2 · Main: 6 × 800m Z3Z4 with 75s rest · Cool-down: 1km · Total: ~10km

Luke Note: The close-grip bench is your tricep strength builder — keep elbows tucked. Last 2 intervals of the tempo run should feel like a 7/10 effort. Control breathing.

Z2 Strictly aerobic. Don't be tempted to push pace after a big D1.

Steady State — 50min
Z2 Last 10 min: slightly increase resistance to push into Z3.

Weighted Pull-Ups (+10kg)4×5RPE 8

Z1Z2 Recovery pace. Flush legs from D3.

Strength — Shoulders & Arms
Seated DB Press4×8RPE 8
Lateral Raise (cable)5×15RPE 7

Incline Run — 30min
6% incline, maintain running form Z2Z3 Alternate: 5min run / 2min walk incline.
D6LONG RUN + ACTIVE RECOVERYRecovery
Cardio — Long Run
Long Run — 14km
Z1Z2 First 10km easy, final 4km with slight negative split. Eat before. Fuel during (gel at 8km).
Active Recovery
Full Body Stretch + Foam Roll — 25min
Priority: calves, hip flexors, quads, ITB. Elevate legs post-run.

Luke Note: Start fuelling your long run. A gel at 8km will transform how the last 6km feel. Build the habit now before distances get serious.

D7LEGS + JUMP ROPE / SWIMHybrid

Jump Rope: 5×3min / Swim: 600m
Z3 Jump rope intervals (30s fast / 30s normal). Or 600m swim alternating stroke styles.

Long Run
Long Run — 16km

06
PHASE 2 DELOAD ↓ Deload
Drop volume 20–25% · Keep loads the same · Short runs only · Prioritise sleep, nutrition, and tissue health
Est. Run Volume
22km
Lifting Days
6days
Long Run
12km
Avg RPE Lifts
6.5
D1–D7DELOAD TEMPLATE — ALL DAYSRecovery
Deload Protocol
All compound lifts: same weight–1 to 2 setsRPE 6
All isolation work: –2 reps per set–1 setRPE 5
Tempo/interval runs: 50% volumeHalf reps
Easy runs: –1 to 2km eachSame pace
Long run reduced to 12kmFull Z2

Luke Note: Phase 2 is demanding. This deload is where the gains are made. Tissue heals, hormones reset, nervous system recovers. Phase 3 will be your best yet — but only if you rest now. No hero sessions this week.

Phase 3 · Weeks 7–9 ACCUMULATION
Phase Focus

Highest training load of the block. Strength reaches RPE 8–9. Long run climbs to 20km+. Tempo efforts become threshold work. Bike includes proper intervals. This is where you earn your fitness — manage fatigue carefully.

07
ACCUMULATION BEGINS
Bring up all loads from deload · Introduce threshold work (Z4) · Long run 18km · First real test of hybrid capacity
Est. Run Volume
38km
Lifting Days
6days
Long Run
18km
Bike
60min
Avg RPE Lifts
8.5
D1CHEST / ARMS + TEMPO RUNHybrid
Strength
Incline Barbell Bench Press5×5 (heavy)RPE 8
Flat Dumbbell Press4×8RPE 8
Cable Fly4×12RPE 7
Close-Grip Bench4×6RPE 8
EZ-Bar Curl4×8RPE 8
Hammer Curl superset Pushdown3×12/12RPE 7
Overhead Tricep Extension3×10RPE 8
Cardio — Threshold Run
4 × 1.2km Threshold Intervals
W/U: 2km Z2 · Main: 4 × 1.2km Z4 hard — 2min recovery · C/D: 1.5km · Total: ~12km

Luke Note: Phase 3 threshold reps should be uncomfortable. You'll feel like you're working, breathing is laboured, but you can hold the pace — that's Z4. That's the sweet spot.

D6LONG RUN + ACTIVE RECOVERYRecovery
Long Run
Long Run — 18km
Z2 Gel at 8 & 14km. Last 3km: push into low Z3 if HR allows. Strong finish = mental training.
Recovery
Stretch + Cold Therapy — 30min
Full body mobility. Ice bath or cold shower. Refuel within 30min of finishing.

Luke Note: 18km is a serious run. Eat carbs the night before. Get 8+ hours sleep. Don't do this on empty.

D7LEGS + JUMP ROPE / SWIMHybrid
Strength

Jump Rope: 6×3min / Swim: 800m

08
PEAK ACCUMULATION — HIGHEST LOAD
Max load week of the block · Long run 20km · RPE 8–9 on all main lifts · Manage recovery obsessively · This week defines your base
Est. Run Volume
42km
Lifting Days
6days
Long Run
20km
Bike
65min
Avg RPE Lifts
8.5–9
W8PROGRESSIVE OVERLOAD FROM WEEK 7Peak
Week 8 Progression Rules
All compound lifts: +2.5–5kg from Week 7RPE 8–9
Isolation work: +1–2 reps per setRPE 7–8
Tempo intervals: 5 × 1.5kmZ4
Easy runs: 7km eachZ2
Long run: 20kmZ2 + final 3km Z3
Bike: 65min structured intervalsZ4 efforts
Jump Rope: 7 × 3min / Swim: 900mZ3–Z4

Luke Note: Week 8 is the hardest week of the entire block. This is not meant to feel comfortable. Sleep 8–9 hours. Eat above maintenance. Take the process seriously — your body needs fuel to handle this load.

09
PHASE 3 DELOAD — ABSORB AND RESET ↓ Deload
Volume drops 25–30% · Keep loads · Short runs only · Body transforms the stress of W7–8 into fitness · Mandatory
Est. Run Volume
24km
Long Run
12km
Avg RPE Lifts
6.5
W9DELOAD PROTOCOL ALL DAYSDeload
Deload Rules
All compounds: same weight, –1 to 2 setsRPE 6
All isolation: –2 reps, –1 setRPE 5
Runs: cut all by 30–40%Z1–Z2 only
No threshold or Z4 workAll easy
Long run: 12km at easy paceZ1–Z2
Extra: 15min daily mobility + soft tissue workDaily

Luke Note: You just completed the hardest phase. Now absorb it. Sleep, eat, stretch. Phase 4 will build on this foundation — show up rested.

Phase 4 · Weeks 10–12 PEAK BASE
Phase Focus

Consolidate all gains. Strength stays heavy but volume peaks out. Running introduces Z5 short intervals. Long run extends to 22–24km. Final week = deload and assessment. You'll end this block a completely different athlete.

10
PEAK BASE — COMING OUT SWINGING
Return from deload firing · Introduce Z5 speed work · Long run 22km · Squats and bench approaching new maxes · This is your best week
Est. Run Volume
44km
Long Run
22km
Bike
65min
Avg RPE Lifts
8–9
D1CHEST / ARMS + SPEED TRACKHybrid
Strength — Chest & Arms (Peak Load)
Incline Barbell Bench Press4×4 (near max)RPE 9
Flat Dumbbell Press4×8RPE 8
Cable Fly Superset Dips3×10/10RPE 8
Close-Grip Bench Press4×5RPE 8
EZ-Bar Curl + Hammer Curl SS4×8/10RPE 8
Overhead Tri Extension3×10RPE 8
Cardio — Track Speed + Threshold
Pyramid Intervals — Track
W/U: 2km easy Z2
Main: 400m Z5 → 800m Z4 → 1km Z4 → 800m Z5 → 400m Z5
Recovery: 2min between each
C/D: 1.5km easy · Total: ~10km

Luke Note: This is your best version of yourself showing up. Peak base, deload absorbed, fresh nervous system. Attack the track session. The Z5 reps should feel fast — trust the process.

D2BACK / SHOULDERS + EASY RUNHybrid
Strength
Barbell OHP4×4 (near max)RPE 9

D6LONG RUN + ACTIVE RECOVERYRecovery
Long Run — Peak Distance
Long Run — 22km
Z2 Gel at 8, 14, 19km. First 16km easy. Final 6km: allow Z3. Strong finish. This is your longest run of the block.
Recovery Protocol
Post-run: Full Recovery Protocol
Refuel within 20min (carbs + protein). Elevate legs 15min. Foam roll + mobility 30min. Cold bath if available. Prioritise sleep.

Luke Note: 22km is half-marathon+ territory. You've built to this. Celebrate it — but then recover like a professional. This is where amateur and elite split apart.

Week 11 Rules
Compounds: attempt +2.5–5kg on key liftsRPE 9
Isolation: +1–2 reps per setRPE 8
D1 Track: 6 × 400m Z5 + 3 × 800m Z4Speed work
Easy runs: 7km eachZ2

Luke Note: Week 11 is your statement week. You're proving to yourself what 10 weeks of consistent training creates. The 24km long run is the crown jewel — trust every mile you've banked.

Cardio — Time Trial
5km Time Trial
Run your 5km as fast as possible. Compare to Week 1 pace. This is your proof of progress.

Luke Note: Record everything. Weight on lifts. 5km time. Long run pace. Write it down — Week 1 you vs Week 12 you. That gap is the result of 12 weeks of discipline. Be proud. Then plan the next block.

D6FINAL LONG RUNAssessment
Block Finale Run
Long Run — 14km (Deload + Reflection)
Z2 Run easy. Reflect on the 12 weeks. Compare pace to Week 1 long run pace — it should feel significantly easier.

Block complete.

Luke Note: You did it. 12 weeks. You are not the same person who started this plan. Rest 5–7 days, then start planning your next block — with higher starting weights, faster target paces, and bigger goals.

ASSESS12-WEEK PROGRESS CHECKPOINTSReview
What To Measure vs Week 1
5km Run TimeTarget: –2 to 4 min
Long Run Pace (same feel)Target: significantly faster
Incline Bench Press MaxTarget: +10–20kg
Back Squat MaxTarget: +15–25kg
Weighted Pull-Up (total = BW + weight)Target: +10–15kg
Resting HRTarget: –5 to 10bpm
Body CompositionLeaner, more muscular
